name: PySTAC / STAC API
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); data access costs depend on STAC catalog provider (AWS, Planetary Computer, etc.)

auth.notes: Depends on STAC catalog: Microsoft Planetary Computer uses token auth; AWS open catalogs are unauthenticated

performance.notes: Catalog search latency ~200ms; actual data download depends on file size and cloud proximity

agent_readiness.notes: Python client for SpatioTemporal Asset Catalogs (STAC) — the standard for satellite/aerial imagery metadata. Search catalogs (AWS Open Data, Microsoft Planetary Computer, Element 84, USGS) by spatial extent, time range, cloud cover, band. Returns Items with links to actual data (GeoTIFF, COG). Works with Rasterio, Xarray, StackSTAC for downstream processing. Used in satellite imagery ML pipelines.

gotchas: Authentication varies by catalog — no universal auth model
STAC spec versions (0.9, 1.0) differ between catalogs
Data downloads not handled by pystac-client — need Rasterio/GDAL for actual access
Spatial queries require GeoJSON or bbox format
best_when: Discovering and searching satellite imagery (Sentinel-2, Landsat, NAIP) by location/time
Building satellite imagery ML pipelines with standardized metadata
Accessing cloud-native raster data from AWS or Microsoft Planetary Computer
Geospatial analytics requiring temporal series of satellite data
avoid_when: Non-satellite geospatial data (no STAC catalogs exist)
Real-time imagery (STAC catalogs typically have processing latency)
summary: pystac-client is the Python client for STAC (SpatioTemporal Asset Catalog) APIs — the open standard for discovering satellite and aerial imagery. Search catalogs like AWS Open Data, Microsoft Planetary Computer, and USGS by location, time range, and cloud cover. Returns metadata with links to Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFFs. Pairs with Rasterio and Xarray for ML pipelines.
